Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-14T20:49:45Z","updated_at":"2024-11-14T20:49:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

In [__init__.pyi](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/44c22637444e67ebcbc49f6a947ab6698dc1c8ea/numpy/linalg/__init__.pyi#L70) the type signature is:

```python
class LinAlgError(Exception): ...
```

But in [_linalg.py](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/44c22637444e67ebcbc49f6a947ab6698dc1c8ea/numpy/linalg/_linalg.py#L74) its:

```python
class LinAlgError(ValueError):
```

This leads to confusing linter errors like:

```text
Bad except clauses order (ValueError is an ancestor class of LinAlgError) (bad-except-order)
```

Because if you ctrl+click in your IDE (to go to source) it takes you to the `__init__.pyi` which is apparently incorrect.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
>>> import numpy as np
>>> np.linalg.LinAlgError.__mro__
(<class 'numpy.linalg.LinAlgError'>, <class 'ValueError'>, <class 'Exception'>, <class 'BaseException'>, <class 'object'>)


And can trigger the linter finding using this code:

```console
ltheisen@laptop ~
$ (dir="$(mktemp --directory)"; trap "rm -rf '${dir}'" EXIT; cat > "${dir}/test.py" <<'EOF'
"""A test"""
import numpy
try:
    raise numpy.linalg.LinAlgError("testing")
except ValueError as err:
    print(f"value error: {err}")
except numpy.linalg.LinAlgError as err:
    print(f"value error: {err}")
EOF
  pylint "${dir}/test.py";)
************* Module test
/tmp/tmp.pRqom291wH/test.py:7:7: E0701: Bad except clauses order (ValueError is an ancestor class of LinAlgError) (bad-except-order)

------------------------------------------------------------------
Your code has been rated at 2.86/10 (previous run: 2.86/10, +0.00)
```
```


### Error message:

_No response_

### Context for the issue:

this only effects my work in that it was surprising and caused consternation.  took about an hour to get to the bottom of this.  fixing the `.pyi` to be inline with the actual source code should be simple and will hopefully remove the problem for future developers.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27768/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27768/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
